title_en: Reception and Use as a Teaching Text
content_en:
Subul as-Salam has achieved widespread recognition as the most useful and pedagogically effective commentary on Bulugh al-Maram, and since Bulugh al-Maram itself became one of the most widely taught hadith texts in the world, the commentary's reach has been correspondingly broad.

The commentary's reception was initially strongest in Yemen and the broader Arabian Peninsula, where as-San'ani's scholarly reputation was highest. It spread from there with the movement of Yemeni scholars and students across the Muslim world, reaching South Asia, East Africa, and Southeast Asia. By the modern era, Subul as-Salam had become a standard reference in Islamic institutions wherever Bulugh al-Maram was taught.

Ash-Shawkani, who came after as-San'ani and was influenced by him, provides important context for understanding the intellectual tradition from which Subul as-Salam emerged. Both scholars shared the Yemeni intellectual tradition of combining rigorous hadith scholarship with critical independence in legal reasoning, and reading their works together illuminates a distinct approach to Islamic jurisprudence that differs both from the madhab-bound traditionalism of the major schools and from the more simplistic anti-madhab positions sometimes found in later reform movements.

For students, Subul as-Salam is an ideal text to study after developing basic competence in Arabic, fiqh, and hadith terminology. As a commentary on Bulugh al-Maram, it provides a comprehensive survey of the most important legal hadiths organized by topic, with explanations that build the reader's understanding of both the hadith content and its jurisprudential context. Reading Subul as-Salam with Bulugh al-Maram gives the student an integrated introduction to legal hadith scholarship that serves as a foundation for more specialized study.

The modern critical edition of Subul as-Salam by Dar al-Hadith, Cairo, along with various other printings, has made the text widely available. Its accessibility in Arabic — as-San'ani writes in clear, precise prose — makes it more immediately readable than some of the older legal-hadith commentaries, and this readability has contributed to its continued popularity in contemporary Islamic education.
title_id: Penerimaan dan Penggunaan sebagai Teks Pengajaran
content_id:
Subul as-Salam telah mendapatkan pengakuan luas sebagai syarah atas Bulugh al-Maram yang paling bermanfaat dan efektif secara pedagogis, dan karena Bulugh al-Maram sendiri menjadi salah satu teks hadis yang paling banyak diajarkan di dunia, jangkauan syarah ini pun menjadi sama luasnya.

Penerimaan syarah ini awalnya paling kuat di Yaman dan Semenanjung Arabia yang lebih luas, di mana reputasi keilmuan as-Shan'ani paling tinggi. Ia menyebar dari sana seiring perpindahan ulama dan pelajar Yaman ke seluruh dunia Muslim, menjangkau Asia Selatan, Afrika Timur, dan Asia Tenggara. Pada era modern, Subul as-Salam telah menjadi referensi standar di lembaga-lembaga Islam di mana pun Bulugh al-Maram diajarkan.

Asy-Syaukani, yang datang setelah as-Shan'ani dan dipengaruhi olehnya, memberikan konteks penting untuk memahami tradisi intelektual dari mana Subul as-Salam muncul. Kedua ulama ini berbagi tradisi intelektual Yaman yang memadukan kajian hadis yang ketat dengan kemandirian kritis dalam penalaran hukum, dan membaca karya-karya mereka bersama-sama menerangi sebuah pendekatan khas terhadap fikih Islam yang berbeda baik dari tradisionalisme terikat mazhab maupun dari posisi anti-mazhab yang lebih sederhana yang kadang ditemukan dalam gerakan-gerakan pembaruan belakangan.

Bagi para pelajar, Subul as-Salam adalah teks ideal untuk dipelajari setelah mengembangkan kompetensi dasar dalam bahasa Arab, fikih, dan terminologi hadis. Sebagai syarah atas Bulugh al-Maram, ia menyediakan survei komprehensif hadis-hadis hukum terpenting yang disusun berdasarkan topik, dengan penjelasan yang membangun pemahaman pembaca tentang isi hadis dan konteks yurisprudensialnya. Membaca Subul as-Salam bersama Bulugh al-Maram memberikan pelajar pengantar terpadu tentang kajian hadis hukum yang berfungsi sebagai fondasi untuk studi yang lebih terspesialisasi.

Edisi kritis modern Subul as-Salam terbitan Dar al-Hadis, Kairo, bersama berbagai cetakan lainnya, telah membuat teks ini tersedia secara luas. Aksesibilitasnya dalam bahasa Arab, karena as-Shan'ani menulis dalam prosa yang jernih dan tepat, membuatnya lebih mudah dibaca daripada beberapa syarah hadis-hukum yang lebih tua, dan keterbacaan ini telah berkontribusi pada popularitasnya yang terus berlanjut dalam pendidikan Islam kontemporer.
content_ar: Subul as-Salam has achieved widespread recognition as the most useful and pedagogically effective commentary on Bulugh al-Maram, and بما أن Bulugh al-Maram itself became one of the most widely taught الحديث texts in the الدنيا, the commentary's reach has been correspondingly broad. The commentary's reception was initially strongest in Yemen and the broader Arabian Peninsula, where as-San'ani's العلمي reputation was highest. It spread from there with the movement of Yemeni العلماء and students across the مسلم الدنيا, reaching South Asia, East Africa, and Southeast Asia. By the modern era, Subul as-Salam had become a standard reference in Islamic institutions wherever Bulugh al-Maram was taught. Ash-Shawkani, who came بعد as-San'ani and was influenced by him, provides المهم context for الفهم the intellectual التراث from which Subul as-Salam emerged. Both العلماء shared the Yemeni intellectual التراث of combining rigorous الحديث scholarship with البالغ الأهمية independence in legal reasoning, and reading their works together illuminates a distinct approach to Islamic jurisprudence that differs both from the madhab-bound traditionalism of the major schools and from the more simplistic anti-madhab positions sometimes found in later reform movements. For students, Subul as-Salam is an ideal text to study بعد developing الأساسي competence in Arabic, الفقه, and الحديث terminology. As a commentary on Bulugh al-Maram, it provides a comprehensive survey of the most المهم legal hadiths organized by topic, with explanations that build the reader's الفهم of both the الحديث content and its jurisprudential context. Reading Subul as-Salam with Bulugh al-Maram gives the student an integrated introduction to legal الحديث scholarship that serves as a foundation for more specialized study. The modern البالغ الأهمية edition of Subul as-Salam by Dar al-الحديث, Cairo, along with various other printings, has made the text widely available. Its accessibility in Arabic — as-San'ani writes in clear, precise prose — makes it more immediately readable than some of the older legal-الحديث commentaries, and this readability has contributed to its continued popularity in contemporary Islamic education.
